utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: elizabeth , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: doing okay . so i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . elizabeth is a 66-year-old female with a past medical history of significant for depression and hypertension who presents for her annual exam . so elizabeth , it's been a year since i've seen you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well . there's been so many events now that we've been able to get vaccinated , i've been able to see my grandkids again , go to more birthday parties , it's been fantastic .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i know . i've ... i mean , we've had some communication over telehealth and that type of thing , but it's not the same as being in-person , so i'm happy to see you today . now tell me a little bit about , you know , we have n't really got a chance to talk about your depression . how are you ... how are you doing with that ? i know we have n't had you on medication in the past because you're on medication for other things . what are your strategies with dealing with it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i've been going to therapy once a week for the past year . um , mostly virtually , um , but starting to get in-person . virtual was a bit of a struggle , but i feel like being in-person with someone really helps me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and do y- do you have a good support system at home ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do . i have my husband and , uh , my kids are right down the street from me , so i'm very lucky .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so that's good to hear .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how about your blood pressure ? did you buy the blood pressure cuff that i-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . told you to ? okay . i know that we've been in communication and your blood pressures have been running okay . the last time i spoke with you , how are they doing since we last spoke ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they've been doing well . i've been using the cuff , um , once a day , and they seem pretty normal .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are you taking the lisinopril that i prescribed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . all right . so i know that you did a review of systems sheet with the nurse when you checked in . you know , i know that you were talking about some nasal congestion . do you have any other symptoms , chest pain , shortness of breath , abdominal pain , nausea or vomiting , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no ? okay . all right . well , i wan na go ahead and do a quick physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so here in the office , you know , your blood pressure looks really good , as does your heart rate . so you are doing a good job managing your blood pressure . so i'm gon na take a listen to your heart and lungs , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so on physical examination , i do n't appreciate any carotid bruits in your neck . on your heart exam i do notice a slight 2/6 systolic ejection murmur , which we've heard in the past , so i think that's stable . your lungs in- are nice and clear , and you have some trace lower extremity edema bilaterally , so all that means is , you know , i agree , you know , maybe we should watch your diet a little bit better , okay ? but i wan na take a look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey , dragon , show me the ekg . and the nurses did an ekg before you came in , and that looks perfectly fine , okay ? so i wan na just go ahead and talk a little about your assessment and my plan for you . so for your first problem , your depression , i think you're doing a really good job with your strategies . i do n't think ... it does n't sound to me like i need to start you on any medication at this time , unless you feel differently .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i'm , i'm , good in that department .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your second problem , your high blood pressure , i agree , everything looks fine here now . i wan na just continue on the lisinopril , 20 milligrams a day . and i want you to continue to watch your blood pressures as well , and if they start rising , i want you to contact me , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and for your third problem , for an annual exam maintenance , you know , you're due for a mammogram , so we'll go ahead and schedule that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , can i take all my medicine at the same time ? does it matter ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . you can set an alarm so that it reminds you to take your medication . but yeah , you can , you can take them altogether .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 66-year-old female with a history of depression and hypertension presenting for an annual exam.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient is managing her depression.
The patient has been attending weekly therapy for the past year and finds in-person sessions more effective than virtual ones.
The doctor asks if the patient has a good support system at home.
The patient has a supportive husband and children living nearby.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient purchased the blood pressure cuff.
The patient monitors her blood pressure daily and reports normal readings.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking the prescribed lisinopril.
The doctor asks if the patient has symptoms including chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting.
Physical exam findings include no carotid bruits, a stable 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, and trace bilateral lower extremity edema.
The EKG is stable and normal.
The doctor decides not to start new medication for depression.
The patient is to continue lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The patient should continue daily home blood pressure monitoring and contact the doctor via the portal if readings are elevated.
Healthcare maintenance
The doctor will order a mammogram for the patient.
The patient asks if she can take all her medications at the same time.
The doctor confirms the patient can take all medications together and suggests using an alarm for reminders.
